news.headline: 'There's emotion attached': Power gun still uncertain on future call
news.body: As his contract end nears, Zak Butters says he needs to 'start having conversations' about his choices

news.headline: Leaders dismiss trade rumours as Blues flag aggressive draft strategy
news.body: Patrick Cripps and Jacob Weitering remain committed to Carlton as Graham Wright confirms the Blues' plan to attack this year's draft

news.headline: McRae slams 'naive' theory over Pendlebury call, defends Grundy exchange
news.body: Craig McRae has hit back at criticism of the club's management of Scott Pendlebury in the lead-up to his record-breaking 433rd AFL match

news.headline: Team whispers: Star draftee left out, Suns' tall call, Bulldogs make call on English
news.body: Stay up to date with all the latest teams news ahead of round 11

news.body: Ryan Angwin is set to miss this week with a hand; line up cover at selection.

news.headline: Pies prepare for the worst after 'sad' injury, defend resting Pendlebury
news.body: Craig McRae says the club suspects Oscar Steene has sustained an ACL injury

news.headline: LISTEN: Premiums to target, best captains, news, more
news.body: Calvin, Roy and Warnie bring you the round 11 teams, latest Fantasy news, best captains and more

news.headline: Final teams locked in as Hawks, Crows meet in Tassie blockbuster. Follow LIVE
news.body: Follow all the action from Thursday night's clash between Hawthorn and Kuwarna

news.headline: TEAMS: Freo star injured, key Blue out, Bombers make four changes
news.body: The teams for Friday and Saturday's round 11 matches are in, plus the squads for Sunday's games

news.headline: Harley tipped to soar on big stage as milestones collide at MCG
news.body: Most of the fans will be there cheering on Collingwood legend Scott Pendlebury, but Harley Reid will be doing his utmost to spoil the party

news.headline: LISTEN: Eagles lean into 'villain' role, Freo on verge of record, Grundy for AA?
news.body: Join Michael Whiting, Nathan Schmook, Emily Patterson and Bharat Sundaresan for AFL Daily

news.headline: Mannagh's heavenly night leads Cats to statement win over Lions
news.body: Geelong records a 41-point win over Brisbane on the back of Shaun Mannagh's five-goal, 30-disposal performance at the Gabba.

news.headline: Hardwick defends Suns staffer amid AFL betting probe
news.body: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says team manager Mark Opie will continue his match-day duties despite being investigated by the AFL integrity unit over his links to a bookmaking firm.

news.headline: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was
news.body: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

news.headline: Rankine says he has 'come a long way' since homophobic slur ban
news.body: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

news.headline: Why this year’s wooden spooner is in the perfect spot to make a bombshell play for Zak Butters
news.body: At the conclusion of the 2026 AFL season, both Zak Butters and Zac Bailey will be restricted free agents and should theoretically have the same rights to explore their options in the league.In a practical sense, they quite demonstrably don’t.

news.headline: ‘Hurt those closest to me’: Neale makes Brisbane captaincy call, confirms separation with wife
news.body: Brisbane dual premiership captain Lachie Neale has said resigning from the position effective immediately was a ‘pretty easy decision’, amid a marriage breakdown with wife Jules.

news.headline: ‘Played here since I was 14’: AFL deny ‘pissed’ Lions star’s Origin application … despite Dees gun being cleared
news.body: The AFL have ruled star Brisbane midfielder Will Ashcroft ineligible to represent Victoria in February’s State of Origin return, after initially clearing him to play in the marquee match.

news.headline: Scott Pendlebury — OUT: this round
news.body: In other team news, Ben McKay is back for Essendon in the Dreamtime at the 'G match after being omitted for the past fortnight, while Collingwood skipper Darcy Moore is in for Scott Pendlebury's record-breaking 433rd game.

news.headline: Dayne Zorko — Injured: this round
news.body: Brisbane will be without injured Ryan Lester and are still missing veteran Dayne Zorko , who was a late scratching last week with "soreness", but have regained premiership forward Ty Gallop .
